trivia: The trijet that built the US domestic network: three engines on the tail, a T-tail, and built-in airstairs for airports with nothing. A few dozen freighters and special ships still earn their keep.
The center S-duct intake above the fuselage is the classic silhouette of 1970s aviation.
funFacts: Its rear airstairs became famous in the D.B. Cooper case.
